seoTitle: Create a Data Hierarchy in Power BI for Drill-Down
metaDescription: Learn how to build a data hierarchy in Power BI so viewers can drill down from summary numbers into detailed data within the same visual.
intro: A good data hierarchy lets one visual do the work of several, letting viewers start at a summary level and drill down into detail without leaving the page. This guide covers defining hierarchy levels, setting them up in Power BI, and visualizing drill-downs cleanly. It's aimed at report builders who want interactive depth without building a separate page for every level of detail.

steps.heading: Define your hierarchy levels
steps.body: Decide the order of drill-down, such as region down to country down to city, based on how your audience naturally explores the data.

steps.heading: Set up the levels in Power BI
steps.body: In the model or fields pane, create a hierarchy by dragging related fields into a defined parent-child structure in the right order.

steps.heading: Visualize drill-downs
steps.body: Apply the hierarchy to a chart or matrix visual and use the drill-down controls to let viewers expand from summary to detail interactively.

steps.heading: Review real-world applications
steps.body: Use hierarchies for common business breakdowns like time (year to quarter to month) or organizational structure (region to team to individual).

steps.heading: Follow beginner tips for effective use
steps.body: Keep hierarchies to three or four levels deep at most, since too many levels make drill-down navigation slow and confusing for viewers.

faq.question: How do I create a hierarchy from existing fields in Power BI?
faq.answer: In the fields pane, right-click a field and choose to create a hierarchy, then drag additional related fields into it in the order you want them to drill down.

faq.question: What's the difference between a hierarchy and just adding multiple fields to a visual?
faq.answer: A hierarchy groups related fields into a structured drill path with built-in expand and collapse controls, while separately added fields don't offer that same interactive navigation.

faq.question: Can I use a hierarchy across multiple visuals in the same report?
faq.answer: Yes, once a hierarchy is defined in the data model, it's available to any visual in the report that uses those fields.

faq.question: Does drilling down affect the underlying data or just the display?
faq.answer: Drilling down only changes what level of detail is displayed in the visual — it doesn't alter or filter the underlying dataset itself.
